item.title: Robust Range Only Beacon Localization Talk
item.description:
At the recent Papers We Love SG Chapter meetup that is held monthly in Singapore we had a nice discussion on robot localization techniques for underwater vehicles as written by the authors of this paper.

Updated slides are here for download.

Things that I liked about this paper are:

  1. The approach taken by the authors to eliminate noisy range measurements and how they reduced it to an eigenvector problem.
  2. A nice linear flow in explaining how the beacons are localized, and how their range measurements are later used to navigate around the beacon-field taking a simple EKF SLAM approach.

The work discussed in this paper will be great to experiment upon to see how well it performs in presence of noise and other uncertainties.

Will jot down some feedback here that I gathered from my friends and myself after presenting the paper. Feel free to comment if you've any suggestion to give. I'll be happy to know about it.

  1. At least three days must be kept aside for preparing the slides and they must be rehearsed at least three times.
  2. For any talk involving robots it shall be taken care, wherever possible, to explain the concepts and implementations through appropriate simulations and/or real-robot demos.
  3. It is better to add some relevant humor before or after discussing technical jargons which usually helps the audience to grasp them quickly.
  4. Finally, one shall never present anything with an empty stomach growling for food.
